conversations.content: Today we studied the Stellar Ring Biopharmaceutical Laboratory Hazardous Chemicals Control Regulations. If an unknown mutant strain is found leaking from a culture chamber...
Unknown mutant strain leak? That's a major biosafety incident! What's the first step?
then spray high-efficiency chemical disinfectant over the contaminated area, then seal all airtight doors of the laboratory.
Disinfectant spray plus airtight door sealing — strict containment! Any other dangerous situations?

If an abnormal sound of rotor disintegration or rupture is detected from the core centrifuge, then activate the equipment's explosion-proof isolation shield, then evacuate personnel within thirty meters of the operating platform.
Centrifuge disintegration? Explosion-proof shield plus 30-meter evacuation — that's a big deal! What's the third scenario?
If a leak is detected from the biowaste treatment pipeline, then pour biochemical neutralization reagent to absorb the waste liquid, then wear level-3 protective suits to repair the pipeline.
Waste liquid leak — neutralize first, then repair with level-3 suits — professional! When any of these happens, what must ultimately be done?

If any of the above situations occurs, then the highest level of biological hazard alarm must be triggered, the entire building's forced ventilation must switch to internal circulation, and no personnel may leave the floor without disinfection.

think: Stellar Ring Biopharma Lab hazardous chemicals control: three anomalies: mutant strain leak → high-efficiency disinfectant + seal airtight doors; centrifuge rotor disintegration → explosion-proof shield + 30m evacuation; waste pipeline leak → biochemical neutralization reagent + level-3 suit repair. Any case → highest biohazard alarm + building forced internal circulation + no leaving without disinfection.
memory: Stellar Ring Biopharmaceutical Laboratory Hazardous Chemicals Control Regulations: If an unknown mutant strain is found leaking from a culture chamber, then spray high-efficiency chemical disinfectant over the contaminated area, then seal all airtight doors of the laboratory; if an abnormal sound of rotor disintegration or rupture is detected from the core centrifuge, then activate the equipment's explosion-proof isolation shield, then evacuate personnel within thirty meters of the operating platform; if a leak is detected from the biowaste treatment pipeline, then pour biochemical neutralization reagent to absorb the waste liquid, then wear level-3 protective suits to repair the pipeline; if any of the above situations occurs, then the highest level of biological hazard alarm must be triggered, the entire building's forced ventilation must switch to internal circulation, and no personnel may leave the floor without disinfection.
